title: Failure Classification, Retry Budgets, Backoff, and Load Shedding · Compiler Case: Transient Permanent Ambiguous
summary: Every exception is requeued immediately, permanent schema failures block healthy work, a database outage causes a retry storm, and attempt counts exist only in process memory. Planned artifact: Add and verify the transient permanent ambiguous increment in failure taxonomy and bounded retry policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify malformed, unauthorized, missing dependency, rate limit, timeout, conflict, transient, permanent, and ambiguous effect failures by evidence.
Set inbox retention from maximum redelivery, replay, audit, privacy, and disaster-recovery windows and test expired-key behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Transient Permanent Ambiguous · Theory Transient Permanent Ambiguous
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 5152 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify permanent and ambiguous outcomes before applying both attempt and age budgets; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then remove the dead-letter binding and expose the resulting retention risk. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Transient Permanent Ambiguous contract for the supplied data.
